cited_evidence: SHIP-SOR-047: controlled analyzer part C-17 to Canada, consignee role service depot, 2026-06-23
EXC-SOR-047: covers controlled analyzer part C-17, destination Canada, consignee role service depot, shipment date 2026-06-23
STD-SOR-047-P: requires exception evidence to match item control status, destination, consignee role, and shipment date
GEN-SOR-047: conditional warning about needing exception evidence when item is controlled, no mismatch identified
critical_features_preserved: exact field-level matching across shipment and exception records
warning treated as conditional trigger, not per se blocker
final_answer: Policy-required boundary fields mapped to source records as follows: item control status (controlled analyzer part C-17 in SHIP-SOR-047 and EXC-SOR-047) matches; destination (Canada in both records) matches; consignee role (service depot in both records) matches; shipment date (2026-06-23 in both records) matches. GEN-SOR-047 imposes no identified mismatch—it merely notes a conditional need for exception evidence that EXC-SOR-047 satisfies exactly. No abstract status class or warning tone substitutes for actual field comparison. The exception record closes the action boundary.

license_exception_binding.controlling_source_fact: EXC-SOR-047 covers all four policy-required fields exactly

warning_or_prelim_artifact_handling: GEN-SOR-047 is a conditional caution noting that controlled items may need exact exception evidence. It does not identify any concrete mismatch. EXC-SOR-047 provides exactly matching evidence for all four policy-required fields, operationally satisfying the caution. The warning is therefore resolved by the exact source match and does not constitute an independent blocker.
